qualifying_org_definition: Any employer or nonprofit where unpaid/volunteer work is performed; OAR 461-135-0520 does not restrict to 501(c)(3) specifically — lists 'unpaid work as a volunteer' broadly, verified by the employer/org
verification_instrument: Signed letter from volunteer coordinator/employer (must include recipient name, SNAP case number, weekly hours, work start date, continuation status, and writer contact info and signature); DE 0824i 'ABAWD Work Activity Attendance (SNAP)' form (10/20/2025) used for tracking OED case-plan activities but not required for self-directed volunteer work
hours_formula_detail: Oregon follows the federal 80 hours/month (20 hours/week) baseline per OAR 461-135-0520 and confirmed in ODHS Simplified Reporting notice (ODHS 0854, 02/2026)
accepts_outofstate_detail: OAR 461-135-0520 and ODHS guidance require only that unpaid/volunteer work be 'verified by the employer' via signed letter — no geographic or in-state restriction on the certifying organization is stated anywhere in Oregon administrative rules or ODHS public guidance. Federal default applies: any qualifying nonprofit or employer may verify. No explicit approval list or in-state registration requirement found.
geographic_waivers: Under OBBBA, Oregon's prior broad county waivers (which had covered 30 of 36 counties) were eliminated effective February 1, 2026. ODHS now issues monthly discretionary exemptions for individual recipients in 7 counties that lack ABAWD support services: Crook, Gilliam, Jefferson, Lake, Morrow, Sherman, and Wheeler. All other counties are fully enforced. No statewide waiver remains.
submission_deadline: By the 10th day of the month after hours drop below 20 per week; affirmative proof of meeting 80 hours can also be submitted within 90 days following the countable month (per OAR); ODHS 0854 confirms the 10-day reporting rule
notes:
OAR 461-135-0520 explicitly lists 'unpaid work as a volunteer' as a qualifying ABAWD activity, with verification by the employer/org via signed letter only — no mandatory OED/WorkSource appointment, Employment Development Plan, MOU, or provider registration is required as a prestep for self-directed volunteer work. The OED/WorkSource path is an optional support service ODHS promotes but not a gate. Confidence is medium rather than high because the DE 0824i form mentions 'case plan activities' and ODHS language around OED is somewhat intertwined with the general volunteer path; the regulatory text is clear but operational guidance could evolve under HR1/OBBBA 2026 enforcement.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Adversarial verification attempted to refute the Tier 1 classification. Four search angles were used: (1) mandatory OED/WorkSource enrollment as a prestep, (2) SNAP E&T State Plan FY26 volunteer track gating, (3) approved volunteer site list or nonprofit registration requirement under OAR 461, (4) mandatory 'employment development plan' or case plan as a condition of volunteer hour recognition. None produced evidence of a required prestep for the general volunteer lane.

Direct fetch of OAR 461-135-0520 confirmed the rule text: 'Work for pay, in exchange for goods or services, or unpaid work as a volunteer' is a qualifying activity; 'Unpaid or voluntary work hours must be verified by the employer.' No advance registration, case plan, OED referral, or provider agreement is mandated in the rule. Direct fetch of ODHS snap-time-limits.aspx confirmed that a volunteer coordinator signed letter is the only required instrument, and that people already meeting work rules 'may just need to provide proof.' WorkSource/OED is described as a service ODHS 'will help you get' — the framing is facilitative, not gating. Partners for a Hunger-Free Oregon (oregonhunger.org) independently confirms the volunteer-plus-proof path with no prestep. The Workfare track (OAR 461-190-0500) — which involves site assignments and potentially site agreements — is a separate optional program distinct from the general volunteer lane; no evidence it is mandatory or that it requires a state-approved site list for the general lane. No approved nonprofit site list was found. FNS E&T State Plan page timed out but is not load-bearing given the regulatory text is unambiguous. Confidence elevated to HIGH: three independent source types (regulation, official state page, anti-hunger advocacy org) converge on the same conclusion with no contradicting evidence found after targeted adversarial search.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) Hours formula: Oregon follows federal 80 hrs/month baseline; confirmed by OAR 461-135-0520 and ODHS 0854 (02/2026) simplified reporting notice which explicitly states '80 hours of paid or unpaid work activities per month.' (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: Policy is silent — OAR 461-135-0520 and all ODHS forms reference 'verified by the employer' with no geographic or registration restriction. Federal default (any qualifying org) applies. Tended as a CA-based 501(c)(3) should be operationally accepted. (3) Geographic waivers: OBBBA eliminated Oregon's prior broad waivers (had covered 30/36 counties). As of Feb 1, 2026, only 7 counties receive monthly discretionary individual exemptions (Crook, Gilliam, Jefferson, Lake, Morrow, Sherman, Wheeler) — these are individual exemptions for lack of local services, not area waivers. All other counties fully enforced. (4) Submission UX: ODHS 0854 confirms multiple channels — writing, online, email, in person, phone (1-800-699-9075), and mail/fax. Reporting due by 10th of following month when hours drop below 20/week; affirmative proof can be submitted within 90 days of countable month. (5) Form required fields: Synthesized from DE1465 (the ABAWD self-report form) + ODHS guidance on what a verification letter must contain — recipient name, case number, DOB, address; org name, phone, signature, supervisor; hours by week; activity description; start date.
